meta.title: C117 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes C117 engine — M270, M133, OM607, OM651. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the OM651 chain risk explained. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes C117 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned C117 engine typically costs £1,200–£9,000 supply only [MB‑QUOTE], depending on engine — the OM607 1.5 diesel is the cheapest at £1,200–£2,800, while the M133 in the CLA 45 AMG runs £5,000–£9,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes C117 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M270 1.6‑litre turbo petrol (CLA 180/200) is the most dependable choice — it has no timing‑chain weakness, and its cooling‑system issues are well‑understood and relatively inexpensive to fix. The OM607 1.5 diesel (Renault‑sourced) also proves robust if serviced properly. The OM651 diesel needs careful chain‑history checking.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes C117?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM651 timing‑chain tensioner is the biggest risk on diesel models — cold‑start rattle is the warning sign. M270 petrol engines suffer from water‑pump and coolant‑pipe leaks, OM607 units develop injector wear, and the M133 in the CLA 45 AMG can experience cam lifter wear. Regular servicing is critical across all engines.

It depends heavily on the engine. Petrol M270 units are generally reliable with routine maintenance. The OM651 diesel is robust but carries a timing‑chain risk that requires proactive inspection. The OM607 (Renault K9K) is simpler and cheaper to maintain, but injector wear is a known pattern. Overall, a well‑maintained C117 can be a dependable premium compact.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes C117 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is an outright “avoid” — but an OM651 diesel without documented chain‑tensioner replacement is the one we consistently advise against. A neglected M133 (CLA 45) without full service history also warrants caution. Always insist on full service history and a specialist pre‑purchase inspection.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my C117 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs include: cold‑start rattle (OM651 chain), persistent coolant loss (M270 water pump), rough idle or hesitation (OM607 injectors), or a top‑end tick that worsens (M133 lifters). If you experience any of these, have the car diagnosed immediately — catching a problem early saves thousands.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes C117 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £10,000 C117, a £3,000 OM651 recon is 30% of the car's value — safe if the car is otherwise sound. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon is 75% — brushing our 70% Mercedes threshold, so evaluate scrap. Use our 70% rule as your guide.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes C117 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance, the M270 petrol can exceed 150,000 miles, and the OM607 diesel is known to reach 200,000+ miles in Renault applications. The OM651's life is limited by the timing‑chain tensioner — addressed proactively, it can comfortably manage 150,000+ miles. The M133 is robust but demands expensive upkeep.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the C117 and X117?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both share the same platform and engines. The C117 is the 4‑door coupé (2013–2019, this page), while the X117 is the 5‑door Shooting Brake estate (2015–2019). Costs vary slightly by body style due to labour access — see the X117's own Generation Page for specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes C117 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen) and in the vehicle documentation. The code is stamped on the engine block — on OM651/OM607 it's on the front left, on M270/M133 it's on the right side. Tell us your variant and registration year, and we'll identify it for you.

hero.h1: Mercedes CLA‑Class C117 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes CLA‑Class C117 Engine Codes — The Complete Database

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: OM651 ratings: 95% confidence, inherited from the Model Page where one exists. M270, M133, and OM607: Expert‑knowledge tier, derived from cross‑referencing specialist and owner sources — confirm against the Model Page before publishing.

overview.intro: The C117 is the first generation of the Mercedes‑Benz CLA‑Class, a four‑door coupé produced from January 2013 to June 2019. Built on the Mercedes‑Benz MFA platform, it shares its underpinnings with the W176 A‑Class and W246 B‑Class. The CLA was positioned above the A‑Class in the Mercedes model range, offering coupé styling with four doors. A facelift in 2016 brought revised bumpers, a diamond grille, and new alloy wheels, but the engine line‑up remained largely unchanged. The C117 is available with front‑wheel drive or 4MATIC all‑wheel drive.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes‑Benz MFA • Predecessor: None (new model line) • Successor: C118 • Sibling body styles in this generation: X117 Shooting Brake estate (2015–2019).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: No C117‑specific enquiry figure is available; OM651 diesel enquiries dominate the broader Mercedes compact‑car parc, with 1,250 UK requests in 2025 [MB‑VERIFIED].

rows.ownershipVerdict: Strong Performer — more power, same upkeep
AMG Tax — high performance, high cost
Budget Diesel — cheap to run, cheap to fix
Watch the Chain — tensioner is the key risk
Best Diesel — torque and economy, if chain is sound

economicsRule.title: The C117 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£5,500 OM651 recon against a £5,000–£12,000 C117 sits at 23–110% of the car’s value. On a sub‑£5,000 example, that brushes our 70% Mercedes threshold — we’ll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well‑kept £10,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 28–55% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C117 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: OM607 1.5 diesel — cheapest recon and simple repairs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C117 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M133 2.0 AMG — AMG parts and labour are eye‑wateringly expensive.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C117 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: M270 1.6 petrol — reliable, no chain risk, and cheaper than the 2.0‑litre.

problems.title: OM651 Timing‑Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.affectedModels: C117 CLA 200 CDI, CLA 220 CDI (OM651 diesel variants).
problems.rootCause: The OM651 has a rear‑mounted timing chain that can stretch over time. Cold‑start rattle is the classic early warning — if ignored, the chain can jump and cause catastrophic engine damage.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold‑start rattle on an OM651, get the chain inspected immediately — a £600–£1,500 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £2,800+ replacement that follows if ignored. On a C117 worth under £5,000, this decision alone determines whether the car stays economically viable.

cta.label: Compare OM651 engine prices for your C117 →

problems.title: M270 Water Pump & Coolant Leaks
problems.affectedModels: C117 CLA 180, CLA 200, CLA 220, CLA 250 (all M270 petrol variants).
problems.rootCause: The plastic water pump housing and coolant crossover pipes can crack with age and heat cycling, leading to gradual coolant loss and potential overheating.
problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £300–£600 water pump replacement is cheap insurance against a £1,500+ head‑gasket repair if the engine overheats.

cta.label: Compare M270 engine prices for your C117 →

problems.title: OM607 Injector Wear (Renault K9K)
problems.rootCause: Injector wear and electrical faults are common on the Renault‑sourced K9K unit, leading to rough idle, poor economy, and increased emissions.
problems.recommendation: If your OM607‑powered C117 develops a rough idle or hesitation, have the injectors tested immediately — a £200–£500 repair is far cheaper than the £1,200+ replacement that follows a full failure.

cta.label: Compare OM607 engine prices for your C117 →

problems.title: M133 Cam Lifter & Turbo Wear (CLA 45 AMG)
problems.rootCause: The high‑output M133 engine can suffer from cam lifter wear and turbocharger issues, particularly if oil changes have been neglected. Ticking from the top end is the classic warning sign.
problems.recommendation: If you’re buying a CLA 45 AMG, budget for a cam lifter inspection and insist on full service history — a £1,500–£4,000 preventative repair is a fraction of the £5,000+ replacement that follows a full failure. This is the price of AMG ownership.

cta.label: Compare M133 engine prices for your CLA 45 AMG →

replacementCosts.h2: C117 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Model Page’s baseline figures — C117 shares its engine bay architecture with the W176 A‑Class and X156 GLA, so costs are inherited directly per the Authority Model, with no divergence note required. OM651, OM607, and M270 costs carried forward from the EM data’s Table A.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are C117 four‑door coupé only. X117 Shooting Brake shares most of these engines but is covered on its own Generation Page, since fitment and labour access vary slightly by body style.
